critical.mandate: Ground truth accuracy is foundational — every other Val workflow depends on it
Never silently delete entries — mark removed files with REMOVED status
Load ground-truth-management skill sections JIT — never pre-load
Behavior must be identical whether called standalone or as sub-step from another workflow

Dual-refresh: when {project-path} differs from {project-root}, BOTH the runtime sidecar at {project-root}/_memory/ AND the committed seed at {project-path}/_memory/ MUST be refreshed. The committed seed MUST preserve the empty-seed invariant (entry_count: 0, estimated_tokens: 0) per E28-S31. Fail-loud if either file is missing.

step.title: Resolve Agent Target
step.action: Parse $ARGUMENTS for --agent value. If --agent is absent, default to "val" for backward compatibility.

Validate agent name against allowed values: val, theo, derek, nate, all. If the agent name is not in the allowed values list: HALT with error: "Unknown agent '{agent_name}'. Valid values: val, theo, derek, nate, all."

Resolve target sidecar path and inventory source files based on agent: - val: sidecar = {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/, inventory = 6-target scan (existing) - theo: sidecar = {memory_path}/architect-sidecar/, inventory = filesystem structure + {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md - derek: sidecar = {memory_path}/pm-sidecar/, inventory = {planning_artifacts}/prd.md + {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md + {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml - nate: sidecar = {memory_path}/sm-sidecar/, inventory = {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml + story files in {implementation_artifacts}/ - all: run sequentially for val, theo, derek, nate (see Step 13)
Set agent_queue = [val, theo, derek, nate]. Proceed to Step 13 for orchestration.

step.title: Initialize Agent Sidecar
step.action: Check if the resolved target sidecar directory exists (e.g., {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ for val, {memory_path}/architect-sidecar/ for theo, {memory_path}/pm-sidecar/ for derek, {memory_path}/sm-sidecar/ for nate)
Create the resolved sidecar directory
Create ground-truth.md in the resolved sidecar directory with empty header containing last-refresh timestamp set to "never"
Create decision-log.md in the resolved sidecar directory with header: "# {agent_display_name} Decision Log" and empty entries
Create conversation-context.md in the resolved sidecar directory with header: "# {agent_display_name} Conversation Context" and empty rolling state

step.title: Determine Refresh Mode
step.action: Check if --incremental flag was passed
Set mode to incremental — only scan files modified since last-refresh timestamp from ground-truth.md header
Set mode to full — scan all targets completely. Full refresh catches deletions and renames that incremental would miss.

step.title: Load Entry Structure
step.action: Load ground-truth-management skill section: entry-structure (JIT)
Use entry-structure format for all ground truth entries written in subsequent steps

step.title: Parse Previous State
step.action: Read existing ground-truth.md from the resolved target sidecar directory
Extract last-refresh timestamp from header
Parse all existing entries into a lookup map keyed by file path for diff comparison
Filter scan targets to only files modified after last-refresh timestamp

step.title: Scan Inventory Targets
step.action: Load ground-truth-management skill section based on mode: full-refresh or incremental-refresh (JIT)

Scan the following 6 inventory targets (existing Val scan — unchanged for backward compatibility), showing section-by-section progress to the user after each target completes.
ALWAYS exclude these directories and files from scanning — they are framework internals, not project code: _gaia/, .claude/, bin/, _memory/, node_modules/, .git/, build/, dist/, .DS_Store, *.lock
Scan {project-path}/**/* (excluding the exclusion list above) Extract: file inventory, directory structure, languages used, entry points Report progress: "Scanning project source files... found N files across N directories."
Scan {project-path}/*.{json,yaml,yml,toml,xml,env.example} (root-level config files) Extract: config keys, settings, dependency declarations Report progress: "Scanning project config files... found N config files."
Scan {project-path}/**/package.json, pubspec.yaml, pom.xml, build.gradle, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, Gemfile, *.csproj (whichever exist) Extract: dependencies, versions, scripts, build targets Report progress: "Scanning package manifests... found N manifests."
Scan {project-root}/docs/planning-artifacts/*.md Extract: artifact name, type, date Report progress: "Scanning planning artifacts... found N artifacts."
Scan {project-root}/docs/implementation-artifacts/*.md Extract: artifact name, type, story key if applicable Report progress: "Scanning implementation artifacts... found N artifacts."
Scan {project-root}/docs/test-artifacts/*.md Extract: artifact name, type, coverage area Report progress: "Scanning test artifacts... found N artifacts."

Scan theo-specific inventory targets (architecture ground truth):
Scan {project-path}/ for directory structure, file types, and project layout. Extract: tech stack, components, module boundaries, entry points, build configuration. Report progress: "Scanning filesystem structure for Theo... found N directories, N files."
Scan {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md Extract: ADRs, architectural decisions, component diagrams, dependency info, integration points, tech stack decisions. Report progress: "Scanning architecture.md for Theo... extracted N ADRs, N components."

Scan derek-specific inventory targets (product ground truth):
Scan {planning_artifacts}/prd.md Extract: functional requirements, non-functional requirements, user stories overview, feature list, product goals. Report progress: "Scanning prd.md for Derek... extracted N requirements."
Scan {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md Extract: epic list, story breakdown, acceptance criteria summaries, dependency graph, sizing data. Report progress: "Scanning epics-and-stories.md for Derek... extracted N epics, N stories."
Scan {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml Extract: current sprint state, story statuses, velocity data, blocked items, completion rates. Report progress: "Scanning sprint-status.yaml for Derek... extracted sprint state."

Scan nate-specific inventory targets (sprint ground truth):
Scan {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml Extract: sprint metadata, story statuses, velocity metrics, blocked items, wave assignments. Report progress: "Scanning sprint-status.yaml for Nate... extracted sprint state."
Scan all story files in {implementation_artifacts}/ matching pattern *-*.md (story files) Extract: story statuses, completion rates, subtask progress, blockers, review gate states. Report progress: "Scanning story files in implementation-artifacts for Nate... found N stories."

step.title: Compare and Detect Changes
step.action: Compare scan results against previous state from Step 5
Classify each entry as: ADDED (new file not in previous state), UPDATED (file exists but metadata changed), UNCHANGED (no changes detected)
For entries in previous state not found in scan results: mark as REMOVED with detection date (e.g., "REMOVED (file deleted, detected 2026-03-19)"). Do NOT silently delete entries.
Skip deletion detection — incremental mode cannot detect deletions. This is a documented limitation.
Load ground-truth-management skill section: conflict-resolution (JIT) if any conflicts are detected between scan results and existing entries

step.title: Write Ground Truth
step.action: Update ground-truth.md in the resolved target agent's sidecar directory with all scan results
Write header with last-refresh timestamp set to current date/time
Organize entries by category appropriate to the target agent
Include verified counts, locations, and structural patterns for each category
Preserve REMOVED entries with their detection dates — do not purge

step.title: Dual-Write Committed Tier 1 Seed (Empty-Seed Invariant)
step.action: Load ground-truth-management skill section: dual-refresh (JIT). Cross-reference memory-management skill section: empty-seed-invariant for the authoritative invariant definition (established by E28-S31).

Resolve the committed seed path for the target agent under {project-path}/_memory/: - val: {project-path}/_memory/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md - theo: {project-path}/_memory/architect-sidecar/ground-truth.md - derek: {project-path}/_memory/pm-sidecar/ground-truth.md - nate: {project-path}/_memory/sm-sidecar/ground-truth.md If {project-path} resolves to {project-root} (project_path: "."), the committed seed path equals the runtime sidecar path. In that case SKIP dual-write — there is only one file and Step 8 already handled it.

action.if: committed seed path == runtime sidecar path

step.action: Skip this step — single-location project layout, no mirror to refresh.
HALT with error: "Committed Tier 1 seed missing at {committed_seed_path}. Fail-loud per E28-S30 AC: both ground-truth.md locations must exist before refresh. Create the empty template first."
Read the committed seed's current frontmatter. Preserve ALL existing frontmatter fields (agent, tier, token_budget, etc.) except update `last_refresh` to the current date.

ENFORCE empty-seed invariant (per E28-S31): after the write, the committed seed MUST retain `entry_count: 0` and `estimated_tokens: 0`. These fields count runtime-populated entries only — they are NEVER raised above zero on a committed seed, regardless of how many entries the runtime sidecar now holds.

Preserve the committed seed's body content below the frontmatter unchanged (this includes the Invariants section and any shipping-template documentation). Do NOT copy runtime entries from Step 8's output into the committed seed.

Write the updated committed seed file. The only diff from the previous committed version should be the `last_refresh` timestamp (and potentially corrections to preserved static content if the human author edited the source).

Post-write verification: re-parse the committed seed's frontmatter and assert `entry_count == 0 AND estimated_tokens == 0`. If either is non-zero, HALT with error: "Empty-seed invariant violated during dual-write: {field}={value}. This is a bug in val-refresh-ground-truth — the committed seed must never carry runtime entry counts."
Report to user: "Dual-refresh: runtime sidecar updated ({runtime_path}, N entries), committed seed timestamp refreshed ({committed_seed_path}, entry_count: 0 preserved)."

step.title: Generate Diff Report
step.action: Generate diff/delta report summarizing changes since last refresh
Include counts by category: added, removed, updated entries
Include total entry count across all categories
Format: "Added: N new workflows, N new agents. Removed: N deleted commands. Updated: N file paths changed. Total entries: N."
Present the full diff report to the user

step.title: Log to Decision Log
step.action: Append the diff/delta report to the target agent's own decision-log.md in the resolved sidecar directory. Route to the correct file based on resolved agent target: - val: {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md - theo: {memory_path}/architect-sidecar/decision-log.md - derek: {memory_path}/pm-sidecar/decision-log.md - nate: {memory_path}/sm-sidecar/decision-log.md Never write to another agent's decision-log.md — this would violate cross-agent write isolation.
Include date, refresh mode (full or incremental), target agent name, and summary
Format: "## Refresh — {date} ({mode}) — Agent: {agent_name}\n{summary}"

step.title: Check Token Budget
step.action: Load ground-truth-management skill section: token-budget (JIT)

Load the correct per-agent ground_truth_budget from {memory_path}/config.yaml based on target agent: - Val: 200K (200,000 tokens) - Theo: 150K (150,000 tokens) - Derek: 100K (100,000 tokens) - Nate: 100K (100,000 tokens) These are distinct from the 300K session_budget — ground_truth_budget controls only ground-truth.md size.
Estimate token count of the target agent's ground-truth.md
If token count exceeds the per-agent budget threshold: trigger archival of oldest REMOVED entries per token-budget skill guidance
Report per-agent usage separately (e.g., "Theo: 42K/150K, Derek: 18K/100K")
Report token usage to user

step.title: Present Results
step.template-output: Ground truth refreshed for the target agent with verified inventory. Output path is resolved at runtime based on --agent parameter (defaults to validator-sidecar). Includes last-refresh timestamp, categorized entries, and REMOVED markers for deleted files.

step.title: Orchestrate --agent all
step.action: Run refresh sequentially for each agent in order: val, theo, derek, nate. Each agent's refresh completes fully (Steps 2-12) before the next begins. No cross-contamination of sidecar writes — each agent writes only to its own sidecar.
On per-agent failure (e.g., missing source file like prd.md): log the error with reason, continue with remaining agents. Do not halt the entire sequence.

After all agents complete: present a combined summary with per-agent status: - Which agents succeeded and their entry counts - Which agents failed and the failure reasons Format: "Refresh complete. Results: Val: OK (N entries), Theo: OK (N entries), Derek: FAILED (prd.md not found), Nate: OK (N entries)."
